title: No config-write Pi tool — in-pi agents cannot author or amend config.json (framework vocabulary)
description: There is no registered Pi tool to author or amend config.json — the framework vocabulary: block_kinds, relation_types, lenses, invariants, status_buckets, display_strings, layers, installed_schemas/blocks. read-config is read-only; project-init does not scaffold config.json at all (defaults/ ships neither config.json nor config.schema.json — empirical: a fresh-init read-config returns null and validateProject is vacuously clean because there is nothing to validate). writeConfig exists as a library surface (project-context.ts, consumed by the orchestrator + the rename engine) with no pi.registerTool twin. So a tools-only in-pi agent cannot register a block_kind / relation_type / lens / invariant, nor set a config.status_buckets override (DEC-0025) — the entire config-driven machinery (DEC-0015/0025) is unestablishable and unrevisable from inside Pi.

evidence.reference: only read-config registered (read-only); no write-config / amend-config tool
writeConfig library surface exists (orchestrator + rename engine consume it); no Pi-tool twin
project-init scaffold omits config.json + config.schema.json; empirical fresh-init: read-config = null, validateProject vacuously clean

DEC-0015 (config-driven substrate dir + vocabulary) + DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral; config.status_buckets override) + DEC-0019/0020 (dual-surface) + DEC-0038 (onboarding) — the Pi-tool twin is missing

impact: Framework vocabulary cannot be created or revised via tools; onboarding (DEC-0038) — which must elicit and write config — has no write surface; config-dependent tools (project-validate, context-current-state, lenses, roadmap, gather-execution-context) degrade to empty/vacuous on any substrate an in-pi agent provisioned. Blocks the consumption arc (FGAP-068 onboarding rewire) from completing on the tool side.
proposed_resolution: Register a config-write Pi tool, the dual-surface twin of writeConfig — AJV-validated against config.schema.json, supporting scoped amendments (add or replace a single relation_type / block_kind / lens / invariant / status_bucket entry) in addition to whole-config write, with atomic write + DispatchContext attestation. Outcome-only; survey the pi-sdk + sibling pi extensions for the canonical shape; coordinate with the onboarding ceremony (DEC-0038), its primary consumer. DEC-0015 + DEC-0019/0020 + DEC-0025.
